engine.name: ik_llama.cpp
engine.backend: RPC client plus four SYCL Level Zero rpc-server workers

patches: RPC socket transactions now take a per-socket mutex, preventing concurrent command/response interleaving.
RPC graph cache access is protected by a mutex.

GGML_SCHED_ASYNC_WAVE=1 now copies split inputs serially, groups only distinct-backend splits with no intra-wave input dependency, runs graph compute in parallel, and serializes completion bookkeeping.
The older per-backend async path is renamed to GGML_SCHED_ASYNC_PER_BACKEND_UNSAFE to avoid accidental use.

baselineGraph.label: safe graph split before wave2
baselineGraph.notes: Graph split completed but was dominated by 503 split/RPC submissions.

runs.label: wave2 flash attention probe
runs.failure: SYCL worker aborted: FLASH_ATTN_EXT unsupported in this RPC graph path.

conclusion: Wave2 makes graph split safe enough to run and gives a small positive p0/n16 gain, but graph mode remains much slower than the corrected layer baseline at 14.292 tok/s. The next meaningful graph work is to reduce the 503 split submissions by changing MiniMax graph construction, especially the cross-backend norm/setup dependency before per-device attention/MoE branches.

localmaxxing.reason: Not submitted because this is an experimental graph-path learning result and is far slower than the current corrected layer baseline. localmaxxing.com was also returning 522 earlier in this session.
